acceptance_targets: [chapter_14_award_01] PASS if the revised sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:507 contains at least one explicit action demand with immediate cost or benefit and one visible response by Nita or Ben before the last paragraph; FAIL if the scene still ends on packets, interpretation, and bonus offers without a new decision.

[chapter_14_craft_01] PASS if each post-school intervention in the revised sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:491 has a distinct page function that can be named by reading it: one names the threat, one imposes a concrete demand, and one changes material stakes; FAIL if any two consecutive interventions merely restate that the scandal is marketable or trust-positive.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:507 contains at least one explicit action demand with immediate cost or benefit and one visible response by Nita or Ben before the last paragraph; FAIL if the scene still ends on packets, interpretation, and bonus offers without a new decision.
findings.problem: After the classroom set-piece, the chapter's last movement mostly receives packets, interpretation, and incentives instead of forcing Nita or Ben through a fresh on-page choice. That leaves the ending as strong setup for the next chapter rather than a fully landed award-level chapter in its own right.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the apartment sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331, intensify the employer contact at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:371, and rework the final bundle beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:475 so one institution makes a concrete same-night ask tied to Leela, the notebook, school attendance, or media access, and Nita or Ben must materially refuse, hedge, lie, or comply on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each post-school intervention in the revised sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:491 has a distinct page function that can be named by reading it: one names the threat, one imposes a concrete demand, and one changes material stakes; FAIL if any two consecutive interventions merely restate that the scandal is marketable or trust-positive.
findings.problem: The final third repeats one institutional point too many times: the response kit, Pilar warning, Saskia call, euphemism checklist, and continuity upgrade all restate that both systems find the clip useful. Because the beats are so similar in meaning, dramatic momentum flattens into procedural reiteration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or differentiate the post-school beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:333, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:371, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:417, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:475 so each one changes the pressure in a different way: dissemination threat, concrete extraction demand, and material leverage should not all be delivered as variants of the same insight.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the ugly overlap between office systems and intimate life: sweat under formalwear, sponsored groceries in the middle of a fight, sex that clarifies nothing, a child noticing coercion through object logic. When choosing between elegant abstraction and embarrassing specificity, choose specificity unless the character is actively lying to themselves. If a scene risks becoming pure farce, restore weight through money, care work, or the body's limits; if it risks becoming solemn prestige fiction, let the absurd machinery stay absurd.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft replaces bodily fact, financial shame, sexual consequence, or emotional cruelty with tasteful summary, rewrite until the cost is present in scene rather than merely reported.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, ritual formality, or character-specific self-control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should move the same way. Follow spectacle with aftermath, ritual with drift, humiliation with quiet, revelation with preparation. Vary scene count, dialogue density, location spread, and whether the chapter advances by confrontation, seduction, dread, comic escalation, or changed understanding.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary English with room for corporate and systems language when characters would actually think or speak that way; every abstract term should be contaminated by bodily or household reality nearby.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third that stays inside the current focalizer's sensory field and only shifts focalization at scene breaks.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate dry compression for comic pressure with longer, accrual-based sentences when shame, fatigue, or desire flood the body. Let jokes land through timing and juxtaposition rather than narrator commentary.
